name: Split the fixed shape into a 64x128 main kernel plus a 64x96 tail kernel

summary.name: Split the fixed shape into a 64x128 main kernel plus a 64x96 tail kernel
summary.hypothesis: Using docs/heuristics.md explicitly, the current 64x96 result now reads as a class-1 plus class-5 problem rather than a plain DRAM ceiling: tensor active is only 28.78%, barrier stall is 29.48%, MIO throttle is 34.18%, long scoreboard is just 0.75%, and DRAM is only 47.58%. Because the fixed N dimension is exactly 7776 = 60 * 128 + 96, most CTAs can run a wider 64x128 main path while a separate 64x96 tail kernel handles the remainder. That should make each staged A/B slice pay for four N fragments per warp on 60 of 81 CTAs, materially increasing math per barrier and per fragment-load episode.
summary.expected_bottleneck: Barrier and fragment-feed overhead in the steady-state K loop; the current CTA already improved residency, so the next step is to amortize `cp.async`, `__syncthreads()`, and `wmma::load_matrix_sync` across more MMA before the next handoff.
code_locations: src/kernels/bf16_gemm_v1.cu:22-57 (tile-shape constants, B-stage footprint, and static guards)
src/kernels/bf16_gemm_v1.cu:158-249 (tensor-core kernel warp mapping, K-loop, and MMA/feed structure)
src/kernels/bf16_gemm_v1.cu:263-289 (launch dispatch that would need a fixed-shape 64x128 main path plus 64x96 tail split)
summary.risk: Moderate to high. The extra accumulator fragment and wider B slab can push the kernel above the current 48 registers/thread and 19.968 KB/block envelope, and the split launch must keep the 96-column tail path simple enough that the main-path gain is not diluted.
summary.stop_condition: Stop if the 64x128 main path materially raises registers above 48/thread or shared memory above the current ~20 KB/block without a clear runtime win, or if tensor activity and barrier stall stay essentially flat versus the current 64x96 kernel.

notes: Build passed. Node A must measure this implementation next.
